summary: Sprawling stage/item decomp PR centered on grZakoGenerator. It converted the zako generator from raw pointer/UNK prototypes into typed config, spawn, data, and item-var structures; implemented several core generator functions; propagated the new optional spawn-desc pointer API across many stage OnStart callsites; and added or improved several stage helper/collision functions. The bot reported +4212 matched code bytes, +56 matched data bytes, 14 new matches, and one small broken match in grCorneria_801E2738.

key_files.why: Core implementation of grZakoGenerator allocation, spawn interpolation, spawn table initialization, per-frame spawn loop, item cleanup callbacks, generator gobj startup, sentinel spawn registration, and cleanup.
Public prototypes changed from UNK/int/void signatures to typed grZakoGenerator_Config, grZakoGenerator_SpawnDesc, Item_GObj, and HSD_GObj signatures.
Local static global narrowed to typed grZakoGenerator_SpawnDesc* and grZakoGenerator_Data* after moving structs to shared gr/types.h.
Introduced grZakoGenerator_SpawnDesc, Spawn, SpawnFunc, Config, Entry, Data; typed stage vars such as Corneria xC8/xCC as grZakoGenerator_Config* and expanded several ground-var structs.
Matched grCorneria_801DD9A8, improved grCorneria_801DDDA8 with typed data layout, and wired Corneria spawn configs through grZakoGenerator_801CA43C.

Typed IceMountainParams.xBC as grZakoGenerator_SpawnDesc, used NULL for disabled generator, added rand_zero helper, and fixed the grZakoGenerator_801CAEB0 argument order in the matched Ice Mountain update.
Matched grRCruise_801FF5B4 and fn_80200460 with typed callback signatures and memory allocation details.
Implemented near-matching grFigureGet_80219B10 coin collision handling and added typed callback parameters and ground-var storage for up to three coin item gobjs/values.
Improved grAnime_801C84A4 by using Ground_801C3FA4, HSD_ForeachAnim masks, setjmp handling, and AObj flag testing.
Added itZako_ItemVars and exposed it through Item.xDD4_itemVar.zako so generator cleanup can use ip->xDD4_itemVar.zako.idx instead of raw offsets.
Aligned item destruction/spawn prototypes with grZakoGenerator_801CACB8 and the generator's item-spawning switch.

what_changed: grZakoGenerator_801CA394 now allocates a typed grZakoGenerator_Config plus a grZakoGenerator_Spawn array, storing spawn_descs, count, callback, x10, gen, and x18 instead of filling a raw 0x1C byte buffer.

grZakoGenerator_801CA43C was implemented as a spawn-position updater: it transforms local spawn endpoint pairs through lb_8000B1CC, checks whether a moving height plane crosses each segment, interpolates a Vec3, then invokes a HSD_Generator-producing callback or updates/kills a persistent generator when x10 is negative.
grZakoGenerator_801CA67C now allocates and initializes grZakoGenerator_Data entries[80] with item IDs i + 0x20 plus a sentinel entry with x0 = -1.

grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4 now scans spawn entries against stage blast zones and camera bounds, decrements respawn delay counters, spawns configured enemies/items for kinds 0x2B, 0x2C, 0x2D, 0x2E, 0xD3, 0xD4, 0xD5, and 0xD9, and records the entry index in Item.xDD4_itemVar.zako.idx.

grZakoGenerator_801CAC14 and grZakoGenerator_801CACB8 were rewritten to use GET_ITEM, itGetKind, the typed zako item var, and the new sentinel layout; grZakoGenerator_801CACB8 remained an unmatched improvement per the bot report.
grZakoGenerator_801CAE04 now returns an HSD_GObj* and takes a grZakoGenerator_SpawnDesc*; dozens of stage startup functions were updated from 0/false to NULL when no spawn-desc table is supplied.
grZakoGenerator_801CAF08 now disables spawning and walks HSD_GObj_Entities->items to remove active generated item kinds, including kind 0x9F.

gr/types.h gained shared grZakoGenerator types and several stage ground-var layout refinements, including grCorneria_GroundVars xC8/xCC as grZakoGenerator_Config* and grZakoGenerator_Data as entries[80] plus sentinel.

Several non-zako stage functions were matched or improved: grCorneria_801DD9A8, grCorneria_801DDDA8, grRCruise_801FF5B4, fn_80200460, grOldKongo_8020F6E4, grHeal_8021F0D8, grOnett_801E40E4, grOldPupupu_8021119C, grFigureGet_80219B10, and grAnime_801C84A4.
Item-side headers and implementations were adjusted so generated item destruction callbacks and spawn helpers use Item_GObj* and typed signatures rather than Ground_GObj casts or UNK_PARAMS.
smart_moves: Moved grZakoGenerator structs out of the static header into gr/types.h so stage structs, stage parameter blocks, and item code can share the same typed layout.

Modeled the old x3C0/x3C4/x3C8 tail fields as a real grZakoGenerator_Entry sentinel, which made grZakoGenerator_801CAEB0 and kind 0x9F cleanup more readable without changing the apparent memory layout.
Changed grZakoGenerator_801CAE04 callsites to NULL instead of 0/false, documenting that the argument is an optional pointer, not a boolean flag.
Replaced raw Item offset access at ip + 0xDD8 with itZako_ItemVars in the item union, making generated enemy cleanup and respawn delay logic reusable by item decomp work.
Used callback typedef grZakoGenerator_SpawnFunc(Vec3*, s32) and updated Corneria effect callbacks to accept the unused s32 argument, avoiding ad-hoc casts.
Kept small matching-oriented helpers such as grZakoGenerator_801CA43C_inline, rand_zero, and rand_inline where they helped codegen while still improving readability.
Typed stage binary-data layouts incrementally, e.g. grCn_Data.indices and grCn_Data.positions, instead of continuing to index into byte pads with casted offsets.
decomp_lessons: When a widely called API is discovered to take a pointer, update every 0/false callsite to NULL in the same PR; this makes future prototype regressions obvious.
For generator-like systems, shared typedefs for config, spawn descs, callbacks, entries, and sentinels can unlock matches across stage, item, and header files at once.
Do not leave item-specific scratch fields as raw xDD4/xDD8 offsets once the owning item-var union is known; adding a small itZako_ItemVars struct improved both generator and item cleanup code.
Collision callbacks in these stage files often need the full typed signature Ground*, s32 joint_id, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, f32; matching one callback can clarify several headers.
Stage data blobs can often be partially typed by anchoring known arrays at offsets, as with grCn_Data.indices at 0x03C and positions at 0x330.

Bot reports can reveal near-miss risk: this PR added many matches but also broke grCorneria_801E2738 by one byte, so adjacent formatting/prototype changes around matched functions should be checked carefully.
naming_conventions: New shared structs use the system prefix grZakoGenerator_: grZakoGenerator_SpawnDesc, grZakoGenerator_Spawn, grZakoGenerator_Config, grZakoGenerator_Entry, and grZakoGenerator_Data.
Item union scratch storage uses item-system naming: itZako_ItemVars and Item.xDD4_itemVar.zako.idx.
Unknown fields remain offset-style names such as x10, x18, xC8, xCC, and xA_b0 when semantics are not proven.
Function symbols retain address-suffixed decomp names such as grZakoGenerator_801CA43C and grRCruise_801FF5B4; no semantic renames were introduced in this slice.
Typed accessor macros GET_ITEM, GET_GROUND, and GET_JOBJ replaced raw user_data or hsd_obj access where evidence supported it.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK annotations in several functions, including grZakoGenerator_801CA43C, grZakoGenerator_801CA67C, grZakoGenerator_801CAF08, grOnett_801E40E4, and fn_80200460.
Kept interpolation as a static inline helper for grZakoGenerator_801CA43C; it computes x/z by linear interpolation and assigns y from the crossing height.

Preserved exact constants relevant to matching and behavior, including 0.0001f for near-horizontal spawn segments, respawn delay 0x708, effect IDs 0x7530/0x7534, and stage checks such as Stage_80225194() == 76.
For random zero checks, grIceMt_801F7080 used an inline rand_zero(s32 n) that returns true when n is zero or HSD_Randi(n) returns zero, matching the previous branch shape more cleanly.
grAnime_801C84A4 uses bit-derived HSD type masks, __setjmp(&grAnime_8049EE40), HSD_ForeachAnim, and HSD_AObjGetFlags(result) & 0x04000000; this is a useful pattern for animation-tree searches.
The item spawn switch in grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4 maps spawn-desc kinds to existing item constructors and stores the generator entry index immediately after creation.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
Automated decomp.dev report: matched code rose to 61.69% (+0.11%, +4212 bytes) and matched data to 37.83% (+56 bytes).

Automated report listed 14 new matches, including grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4, grZakoGenerator_801CA43C, grZakoGenerator_801CA67C, grOldKongo_8020F6E4, grCorneria_801DD9A8, grRCruise_801FF5B4, fn_80200460, grZakoGenerator_801CAF08, grZakoGenerator_801CAE04, and grHeal_8021F0D8.
Automated report flagged one broken match: grCorneria_801E2738 changed from 100.00% to 99.82% by -1 byte.

Automated report also showed near-matches/improvements, notably grFigureGet_80219B10 at 99.18%, grZakoGenerator_801CACB8 at 91.68%, grAnime_801C84A4 at 97.92%, grOnett_801E40E4 at 98.04%, and grOldPupupu_8021119C at 79.22%.
follow_up_queries: Why did grCorneria_801E2738 regress by one byte after this PR?
Can grZakoGenerator_801CACB8 be brought from 91.68% to 100% by refining the item cleanup/sentinel logic?
Can grFigureGet_80219B10 be matched by adjusting duplicate coin tracking, CollData flag typing, or local variable lifetimes?
Can grAnime_801C84A4 reach 100% by refining the result NULL path or HSD_AObjGetFlags call ordering?
Search for remaining stage parameter blobs that should use grZakoGenerator_SpawnDesc instead of int/bool placeholders.
Audit grZakoGenerator_801CA8B4 item kind constants and constructor prototypes for opportunities to replace numeric kinds with enums.
